I’ve been using the suitcase trolley for a few days now, and overall it’s performing well with no major issues so far. The build, storage space, and usability seem satisfactory for regular travel needs.

However, one concern is with the trolley wheels. The wheels shown in the product images on the Amazon listing appear different from the ones actually delivered. The quality of the wheels also doesn’t seem to fully match what was portrayed online, which is a bit disappointing.

Aside from this mismatch, the product is functioning fine at the moment. I hope the wheel durability holds up over time. Would recommend the seller to provide more accurate images and details to avoid confusion for buyers.

Quality & Durability: it's Aristocrat. Enough said.

Size: Perfect for packing all clothes and other items for a single person for a 3-5 days tour anywhere, leaving enough space to pack some more on the way back (provided, the person packing knows how to pack).

Value for Money: Costs less than 30% of MRP. Case settled.

Appearance: It looks exactly like a bag! Add to that the Aristrocat logo in bold, sturdy handles and you have an idea.

Ease of use: Well... it's a 'bag'. It's easy if you have known any bag in your entire life.

5 stars out of 5. The perfect choice for a single traveller or even a couple for a couple of days.

I recently purchased the American Tourister Ivy 78 Cms Large Check-in Polypropylene (PP) trolley, and I must say that I am thoroughly disappointed with its performance. Despite its promising features, this suitcase failed to meet my expectations in several key areas.

First and foremost, the build quality of this trolley is subpar. The polypropylene material used feels cheap and flimsy, making me question its durability. Within just a few uses, I noticed visible scratches and scuff marks on the surface, which is unacceptable for a product that is meant to endure the rigors of travel.

Furthermore, the wheels on this trolley are a major letdown. They lack smoothness and maneuverability, making it a struggle to navigate through crowded airports or uneven surfaces. The wheels frequently jammed and became stiff, adding unnecessary frustration to my travel experience. I expected better performance from a reputable brand like American Tourister.

Additionally, the handle mechanism was poorly designed. It felt flimsy and had a noticeable wobble, which made it uncomfortable to pull the trolley. I had concerns about its longevity and worried that it might break during my travels. A reliable handle is essential for a check-in suitcase, and the American Tourister Ivy failed to deliver in this aspect.

Lastly, the overall aesthetics of the trolley were uninspiring. The design lacked innovation and appeared outdated. For the price I paid, I expected a more stylish and modern look. It is disappointing when a product not only underperforms in terms of functionality but also fails to make a positive impression visually.

In conclusion, the American Tourister Ivy 78 Cms Large Check-in Polypropylene (PP) trolley fell short of my expectations. Its poor build quality, subpar wheels, flimsy handle, and unappealing aesthetics make it difficult for me to recommend this product. I regret my purchase and would advise potential buyers to explore other options before settling for this particular suitcase

The bag looks below average, and I have some doubts about whether it is original because the finishing quality feels quite average. I haven’t used it for travel yet, so I can’t fully comment on durability, but the handles seem very delicate and I’m not sure how well they will hold heavy weight. The packaging was also just average, nothing special.
The product is good. I purchase large size and on the first look the material quality and zip quality seems good. I want to see how well it be good for scratches.

The set is good, colour is nice and there are no scratches or anything.
The overall build quality of the bags and the quality of the wheels and the handles feels a bit weak/flimsy but at this price point can't really complain as in the stores the sets start from 13k onwards.
If anyone wants a decent set of bags in a limited budget then you can definitely purchase this set. It is definitely value for money.

I recently purchased the American Tourister Ivy 78 Cms Large Check-in Polypropylene (PP) trolley, and I must say that I am thoroughly disappointed with its performance. Despite its promising features, this suitcase failed to meet my expectations in several key areas.

First and foremost, the build quality of this trolley is subpar. The polypropylene material used feels cheap and flimsy, making me question its durability. Within just a few uses, I noticed visible scratches and scuff marks on the surface, which is unacceptable for a product that is meant to endure the rigors of travel.

Furthermore, the wheels on this trolley are a major letdown. They lack smoothness and maneuverability, making it a struggle to navigate through crowded airports or uneven surfaces. The wheels frequently jammed and became stiff, adding unnecessary frustration to my travel experience. I expected better performance from a reputable brand like American Tourister.

Additionally, the handle mechanism was poorly designed. It felt flimsy and had a noticeable wobble, which made it uncomfortable to pull the trolley. I had concerns about its longevity and worried that it might break during my travels. A reliable handle is essential for a check-in suitcase, and the American Tourister Ivy failed to deliver in this aspect.

Lastly, the overall aesthetics of the trolley were uninspiring. The design lacked innovation and appeared outdated. For the price I paid, I expected a more stylish and modern look. It is disappointing when a product not only underperforms in terms of functionality but also fails to make a positive impression visually.

In conclusion, the American Tourister Ivy 78 Cms Large Check-in Polypropylene (PP) trolley fell short of my expectations. Its poor build quality, subpar wheels, flimsy handle, and unappealing aesthetics make it difficult for me to recommend this product. I regret my purchase and would advise potential buyers to explore other options before settling for this particular suitcase.
